context: You already know the pieces - now use them to tell your own story. The most common thing you do with a new language is introduce yourself: who you are, how old you are, and where you live. Three small patterns carry almost all of it.
explanation: Everything starts with three verbs you already met. soy = I am, for who or where you are from: soy Preben, soy de Bergen. tengo = I have, and Spanish uses it for age: tengo treinta y seis años (literally "I have 36 years"). vivo en = I live in: vivo en Bergen. Learn to swap the last word and these three sentences already describe your whole life.

examples.en: I am from Bergen, in Norway
examples.note: soy de + place = I am from somewhere.
tengo ... años, not "soy" - Spanish HAS years.

examples.es: vivo en un apartamento en el centro
examples.en: I live in an apartment in the city center
examples.note: vivo en + place; en el centro = in the center.
es describes what something is like.

examples.es: esta rodeada de montañas y llueve mucho
examples.en: it is surrounded by mountains and it rains a lot
examples.note: esta (located/state) rodeada de montañas - a very Bergen sentence.
